sous_titres.text: "THERE IS A BRIDGE CALLED SIRÁT THAT LINKS HEAVEN AND HELL".
"THAT ITS PASSAGE IS NARROWER THAN A STRAND OF HAIR,"
You think she's here in Morocco.
- We'll look. I keep the paper. - Take the...
I’ll keep going for a bit, okay?
Esteban, what are you doing, kid? What’s going on?
You can speak Spanish if you want.
It's been five months since we heard from her.
We were told we might be able to find her here, at this festival.
I don’t know her at all.
Look, there’s another do in the desert.
We have orders to evacuate this place.
As of now there's a state of emergency.
All Europeans are ordered to get into the vans.
This is a restricted area as of now.
Approach the vehicles in an orderly fashion.
We don't bother anybody! We're only dancing!
- What’s wrong with you? - Out of the way.
- Just for a wee. - What did I say?
What? You're going to shoot me for taking a piss?
- Get in the van. - Take it easy.
Isn’t that the girl from before?
The one going to the do.
Let's get out of here! Come on, hurry!
Yes. We want to go to the do you’re heading to.
Not a good idea, as you know.
We want to find my daughter. She might be there.
Follow us? Is this a joke?
Fuck, they want to follow us.
I don’t think you understand what you’re in for.
You do. You could turn back.
explosions in various cities through the country.
Attacks are ongoing on at least four fronts.
The military airport was attacked. There are clouds of smoke.
Bombings have been reported both in the east and the west of the country.
Endless lines of civilians head for the borders. Nobody is any longer...
Things must be getting out of hand.
Let's hope there’s petrol at the next one.
It won't do. It's not enough money.
- Help us please. - It's very expensive.
I get it, but otherwise it's not worth it for us.
What you have here is only enough for one can.
But it's not enough for three.
- Is this okay? - Come on, Afaque.
- Three hundred. - Offer it to them.
Don't say anything. Give me the money.
I think it's enough with this.
- You're welcome. Come on. - Thanks, many thanks.
Like that. Okay, now can you put...
I asked for more photos of the daughter.
She looks a lot like Esteban.
- The door? - The door’s there. It’s this.
- It should line up with the van. - Sure.
Okay. It's this one. Got it. This one.
Can you picture them in the desert?
you snored louder than Pipa did.
I was out like a log.
You were like a duet, you know?
With you, we were a trio, right?
our friends in the bus wouldn't sleep all night.
- It's touching the ground, it won't do. - Calm down. We’ll see.
Did you see the road up ahead?
- Did you see how steep it is? - What do you want me to do?
Tell me! What do I do?
Calm down, Luis. I'll fix it for your car. Hang on.
Go, Luis, okay? Come on, easy does it.
Isn't it pretty? Isn't it cute?
Fuck. I see your tan line.
- Here. There's a tan line? - Just there.
I’ll check further up. Or what do I do?
- You drive, Steff. - Come on, Lu.
How do we get out of here?
It was hard to turn around.
- No, it’s all yours. - What then?
We don't have a lot of supplies.
But you always tell me not to be stingy.
It’s not about being stingy, it’s about being prepared.
But we can still give them a little.
it's going to be a long trip, you know?
All right, but let's be nice. They gave us some food.
Okay. Can you put it on the table? I mean, in the kitchen.
- What's that? - Sit down for a bit.
This one blew out at the do. When that happens, they just toss them.
But I love how it sounds.
They all sound equally noisy to me.
It's never the same sound, see?
You never know if this is the last sound it produces.
Sure. You don't listen to it. You dance to it.
I said, my daughter says the same.
- Jade, bring some milk! - Pipa. What happened?
- Are you okay? - Where was she?
- Pipa, wake up. - What's the matter?
- What happened? - She probably ate poo with LSD.
How would you know that it was mine?
I know 'cause I shat in my truck last night, mate.
- I'll give her some milk. - I'm sure it's yours.
- It could even have been Josh. - There, very good.
Stop lying! You had a shit last night.
Bury it at least or put a stone over it.
Right. You shit in your truck in the desert?
Stop lying, Bigui. You know it was yours.
Bury it next time. You never do that.
You think you are the Desert Queen. This is nuts.
- And you call me a liar. - That's what you are.
I swear I didn't shit in the desert.
Will you shut your gob, please?
This happens a lot, okay? She'll be fine.
We are witnessing a moment of great confusion.
The nations are aligning with either of the sides.
The latest events will have an impact on the world as we know it.
With me is the representative of the NATO Secretary-General,
Mr Schulz, is there still hope at this critical stage?
- You also wanna play, don't cha? - Attack!
- Did birdie take flight? - Don't mess with Bigui!
Enough. It's going to get bumpy!
Yes. Tonin, Jade, Josh and Steffi.
But don't you miss your old family?
I like my current family better.
I think it's very beautiful that you look for her.
Yeah. Why did she run away?
She didn't run away. Mar is an adult. She left.
I think she'll be happy when she sees us.
I have a song for you.
I wrote a letter to you Perhaps you'll read it
To go to war By Wednesday evening
Dear Mr President I don't want to go
I'm not here on earth To kill poor people
I don't say this to upset you
I have to tell you My mind's made up
In the south, close to Mauritania.
No, they have seals. They're called monk seals.
I came to ask if I can have some petrol.
I have a quarter tank left, but I'd rather have some now.
Do you drive with the air con on?
All right. We've still got some reserves.
It wouldn't be bad if you made them communal.
We keep all the food in the 911.
Bring yours. We'll have a common pot.
No idea, but it doesn't look good.
I'd stay on the mountain road.
Check, check, check. Now we're taking this road.
But wouldn't it be easier if we drove through those gorges?
No idea. We're southbound either way.
So, has World War III broken out?
Is this what the end of the world feels like?
No idea what it feels like, Bigui,
but the end of the world started long ago.
No idea. Have a look outside.
I think I fell into a sinkhole.
I can't see a thing, mate.
I know nothing about this stuff.
- There's only one on this. - It's all I have.
Where is it? Look, it's here.
Go! Keep coming, hit the gas!
A little more. We're almost there.
We have to go down! We have to go down to get him!
- Let me see him! - You can't.
- We have to get him! - We can't go down!
- Let me see him! - No, all right?
- We'll get help. - Let me see him.
- Let go... - Not too close, Luis.
I saw him down there, totally crushed.
We have to get help, Jade.
When he's comes out of shock, it'll be grim.
Wait, brother! We need to find a village!
Someone has to show up who can help us.
Go on, turn it up till it explodes!
I keep seeing Tonin exploding in my head.
I've always wanted to go out in a blaze of glory,
Do you know what my father said before he died?
I couldn't say goodbye to my father.
"Pull the brake" are the last words Esteban heard.
Tonin saw Esteban's body when he looked over the edge.
We didn't know how to tell you.
I don't want him to stay there.
No idea how we didn't explode earlier.
It must be full of mines here.
We have to reach that mountain.
Go the other way! The other way, fuck!
How many meters to the rock?
Come on, Josh. We're going with closed eyes.
